title: spiraea ulmaria subsp. denudata
intro: Spiraea ulmaria subsp. denudata, also known as Meadowsweet, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the rose family. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is rich in salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, making it beneficial for relieving pain, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. Meadowsweet can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried herb, and is often used to treat various health conditions, including headaches, menstrual cramps, and respiratory issues.
